proposed_from.topic: Sharp inflation frontier for recompute-based conservative variance estimation under local interference. Setting: n-cycle, radius-M local interference (N_i={i-M..i+M}), Bernoulli(1/2) design, IPW direct-effect estimator, deterministic potential outcomes. Focal object: the minimax excess inflation R*(M,n) = inf over the ENTIRE Park-Wager (2604.24017) recompute class of rules (mu,g) -- mu any index-sampling law with S independent of W, g any fixed measurable map of the OBSERVED data (S, W_{-S}, {Y_i : N_i cap S = empty}) that must serve the whole class -- of sup over Y in Ycal_n(M;c0,C0) of E[Vhat]/Var(tauhat), where Ycal_n imposes radius-M interference, constant direct effect tau in [-1/2,1/2] (the Neyman-tight class, so the irreducible Neyman floor vanishes and the entire excess is the framework's own price), |Y_i| <= C0, and the UNIFORM ARC-VARIANCE SANDWICH c0|I| <= Var(sum_{i in I} psi_i) <= C0|I| for every arc |I| >= 2M+1. KERNEL (thin, precise conjecture): R*(M,n) = 1 + Theta(sqrt(M/n)) for 1 <= M <= n^{1-delta}, ATTAINED by the anchor's own uniform-random-block rule at L* asymp sqrt(Mn) with the M-padded recompute proxy; (C1) upper bound for that rule, (C2) converse for EVERY (mu,g) in the class. Converse content: oracle half >= cM/L by Fourier/isoperimetry on the cycle via Lemma 10 (the Fourier support sits on windows of diameter <= 2M+1; the inflation is |M-neighbourhood of S|/|S|, so only long components beat it -- and they enlarge the deletion set D); proxy half >= cL/n by a Le Cam two-point argument on tau (g never sees the deleted units' outcomes, and must use a NUISANCE-RICH indistinguishable subfamily, NOT the bare parametric witness, which reveals tau). Pre-anchor: Park-Wager 2604.24017 has ONLY Lemma 11 -- one-sided monotonicity of the ORACLE half, pointing the wrong way -- plus proxy heuristics, and reports its U-shape in L empirically with oracle-tuned L. Our theorem is not Harshaw-Middleton-Savje 2112.01709 because that is a QUALITATIVE Loewner-order admissibility characterization over QUADRATIC-form bounds under CORRECTLY SPECIFIED exposure mappings, with a generically-infinite admissible frontier, no rate, no interference radius, and no bound on the MAGNITUDE of unavoidable conservativeness -- misspecified exposures is its own stated open problem. Not Lin-Ding 2510.22864 / Gao-Ding JEcon 252 because those are pointwise-in-bandwidth conservativeness with no optimality and no converse. Why non-trivial: the whole literature has upper bounds only; the converse must hold for EVERY index-sampling law including disconnected and multi-scale ones. Why promising: the M=0 case is exactly solvable (inflation = n/(n-L) = 1 + L/(n-L), optimum L=1, recovering the classical Jackknife) and pins the L/n proxy half, while the anchor's own Prop 3 prefactor (L+2M)/L pins the 1+2M/L oracle half. Reject if the frontier collapses to a routine optimization of Prop 3 without an all-rule converse, if a proxy exploiting the constant-effect restriction imputes deleted units better than n^{-1/2}, or if a multi-scale update set beats 1+cM/L at equal deletion cost.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The nearest in-repository lines are the downgraded effective-exposure lower-bound proposal and the ego-cluster completion-envelope proposal. The former concerns information loss from overlapping exposures but does not define or optimize the Neyman-Jackknife recompute class. The latter asks which observable quadratic envelope is minimal, rather than quantifying the excess inflation of assignment-deletion recomputation. Neither contains \\(R_n^\\star(M)\\), an all-update-law lower bound, or the cycle geometry-proxy tradeoff.","published_axis":"ParkWager2026NeymanJackknife is the closest published comparator: it proves expected conservativeness for any fixed admissible pair and analyzes a uniform block, but it does not take an infimum over update laws and measurable proxies or prove an optimal inflation rate. HarshawMiddletonSavje2026OptimizedVariance is adjacent because it studies minimal conservativeness, but its quadratic Loewner-admissibility frontier has no local-radius rate, no all-rule proxy class, and no magnitude lower bound. The precise proposed advance is a minimax expected-inflation frontier, not a reformulation of either procedure."}
seed_list: A sharp all-rule recompute-inflation frontier on a locally interfering cycle: the best conservative rule has excess order \(\sqrt{M/n}\).
A cycle-isoperimetric oracle frontier: update-set geometry alone forces an \(M/L\) inflation cost at effective update scale \(L\).
A deleted-outcome modulus: every schedule-uniform observable proxy incurs an \(L/n\) loss under nuisance-rich constant-direct-effect schedules.
A uniformly valid Wald-coverage theorem for a tuned padded-block Neyman Jackknife under growing local interference.
A minimax bandwidth frontier under approximate neighborhood interference balancing decay truncation and recompute inflation.
A fractional-chromatic lower bound for design-based confidence length on genuinely overlapping exposure graphs.
A minimal-conservativeness characterization comparing completion envelopes and recompute rules for ego-cluster exposure contrasts.
An exact \(M=0\) characterization of which assignment-independent update laws reproduce the classical jackknife frontier.
A data-independent multiscale update design with an optimality certificate for conservative variance under graph interference.

seed_details.one_line: A sharp all-rule recompute-inflation frontier on a locally interfering cycle: the best conservative rule has excess order \(\sqrt{M/n}\).

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: The Neyman Jackknife gives expected conservative variance estimates from an update law and an observable recomputation proxy.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M11+M7, not routine block optimization: the lower bound ranges over every update law and proxy.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from optimizing the anchor's block prefactor.
seed_details.rebuttal: The claimed converse must exclude disconnected and multiscale update laws and nuisance-adaptive proxies, which the prefactor does not represent.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single periodic schedule cannot lower-bound every proxy.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The question requires a nuisance-rich indistinguishable family inside the stated schedule class.

adversarial_referee.instance: Park and Wager already optimize the tradeoff.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: They establish fixed-rule formulas and numerical tuning, not an infimum over all laws and proxies.

adversarial_referee.instance: The update law and proxy class is underspecified.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The core defines one schedule-uniform measurable proxy and an assignment-independent positive-gap update law.

seed_details.one_line: A cycle-isoperimetric oracle frontier: update-set geometry alone forces an \(M/L\) inflation cost at effective update scale \(L\).

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: Lemma 11 expresses oracle inflation through Fourier support and update-set intersection probabilities.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M11 component, not a full frontier without a proxy half.
seed_details.referee_objection: It is just Lemma 11 with intervals substituted.
seed_details.rebuttal: A sharp isoperimetric lower bound over arbitrary random subsets is absent, but alone it is not a complete paper.

adversarial_referee.instance: Geometry is an intermediate lemma, not a causal kernel.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Retained only as a component of Seed 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Fourier formula is already published.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The all-law extremal geometry is new, but insufficient alone.

adversarial_referee.instance: One Fourier mode avoids genericity.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would require a uniform schedule region; otherwise it is dropped as standalone.

seed_details.one_line: A deleted-outcome modulus: every schedule-uniform observable proxy incurs an \(L/n\) loss under nuisance-rich constant-direct-effect schedules.

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: The proxy sees outcomes only outside the deletion set and larger update sets make approximation more difficult.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M11 lower-bound component with a Le Cam functional modulus, not a bare missing-data analogy.
seed_details.referee_objection: Constant direct effects reveal the deleted mean from retained units.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only a nuisance-rich family that agrees on retained observations can establish the required modulus; this is why it is retained as a candidate rather than asserted.

adversarial_referee.instance: A sample-mean proxy is not minimax among measurable maps.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposed modulus quantifies over all measurable maps.

adversarial_referee.instance: A two-point parametric tau family is distinguishable.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The handle explicitly requires nuisance-rich alternatives.

adversarial_referee.instance: The observation channel is not formalized.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The sigma-field in the converse handle fixes the channel.

seed_details.one_line: A uniformly valid Wald-coverage theorem for a tuned padded-block Neyman Jackknife under growing local interference.

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: The framework guarantees conservativeness in expectation and asks when conservativeness in probability holds after choosing a block length.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M12, not an assumed CLT: it needs a dependency-graph numerator bound and lower-tail variance control.
seed_details.referee_objection: Expectation conservativeness does not imply coverage.
seed_details.rebuttal: That is the precise unresolved lower-tail problem, but it is downstream of the sharper frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: The anchor already gives conservative variance.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It gives expectation-level conservativeness, not uniform studentized coverage.

adversarial_referee.instance: A numerator CLT alone proves nothing about studentization.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: A variance lower-tail theorem is separately required.

adversarial_referee.instance: Tuning selection is undefined.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would require a fixed or split-sample tuning convention before promotion.

seed_details.one_line: A minimax bandwidth frontier under approximate neighborhood interference balancing decay truncation and recompute inflation.

anchor_paper.author: Gao and Ding
anchor_paper.claim: The paper gives conservative network covariance adjustment but leaves bandwidth validity and alternatives unresolved.
anchor_paper.location: Discussion as recorded in the gaps context.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M11, but adds a decay envelope absent from the fixed-radius kernel.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is standard bias-variance bandwidth tuning.
seed_details.rebuttal: A matching all-rule lower bound over observable recompute proxies is nonstandard, but the model expansion is substantial.

adversarial_referee.instance: Approximate interference changes the problem entirely.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Retained as a separate seed, not merged into Seed 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: An upper oracle inequality lacks optimality.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: A lower bound is required for survival.

adversarial_referee.instance: The decay class is unspecified.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs a named ANI envelope before drafting.

seed_details.one_line: A fractional-chromatic lower bound for design-based confidence length on genuinely overlapping exposure graphs.

anchor_paper.author: Sandoval et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Block examples yield effective-sample-size confidence bounds, leaving genuinely overlapping neighborhoods unresolved.
anchor_paper.location: Prior-proposal excerpt in the gaps context.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M7+M12, requiring an overlapping graph family rather than disjoint clique witnesses.
seed_details.referee_objection: Fractional chromatic number may be only an upper-bound artifact.
seed_details.rebuttal: The required fuzzy Assouad construction directly tests necessity, but it is less tied to recomputation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Clique blocks reproduce ordinary rare treatment.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed requires genuine overlap.

adversarial_referee.instance: No graph family is specified.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need a named bounded-overlap family.

adversarial_referee.instance: This is not a recompute variance result.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Kept only as an adjacent alternative.

seed_details.one_line: A minimal-conservativeness characterization comparing completion envelopes and recompute rules for ego-cluster exposure contrasts.

anchor_paper.author: Harshaw, Middleton, and S\u00e4vje
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Admissible quadratic conservative bounds form an optimization frontier.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M4, requiring a domination theorem rather than sharpness by definition of a completion class.
seed_details.referee_objection: The estimator class is arbitrarily selected.
seed_details.rebuttal: A viable version must define an observable quadratic class and prove cross-class domination or nonrepresentability.

adversarial_referee.instance: Observable quadratic rules are not pinned down.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs a fixed matrix cone.

adversarial_referee.instance: Harshaw et al. already optimize bounds.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Only if completion and recompute classes are formally incomparable.

adversarial_referee.instance: No rate or causal target is singled out.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Caps at subfield absent a quantitative frontier.

seed_details.one_line: An exact \(M=0\) characterization of which assignment-independent update laws reproduce the classical jackknife frontier.

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: The framework recovers a Neyman-like estimator under SUTVA.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M6, but likely a technical calibration rather than a new field-level kernel.
seed_details.referee_objection: It is a classical jackknife exercise.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection lands as a standalone project; retain only as a sanity reduction.

adversarial_referee.instance: SUTVA jackknife theory is mature.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands; not a surviving angle.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity over all update laws is artificial.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands; not a standalone contribution.

adversarial_referee.instance: No interference phenomenon remains.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands; used only for calibration.

seed_details.one_line: A data-independent multiscale update design with an optimality certificate for conservative variance under graph interference.

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: The update law is a user-specified choice that controls the Poincar\u00e9 bound.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M13, but the objective depends on unknown schedules and needs a minimax certificate.
seed_details.referee_objection: The design is merely a reparametrization of the update law.
seed_details.rebuttal: It survives only if a minimax saddle point or approximation ratio is established; otherwise Seed 1 is sharper.

adversarial_referee.instance: A multiscale law is asserted good without certification.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: A saddle or lower bound is mandatory.

adversarial_referee.instance: The design criterion is schedule dependent.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use the same minimax class as Seed 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: It duplicates the all-rule frontier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It is subsumed unless it yields a distinct optimizer.

proposed_from.literature_map: Park and Wager (2026) formulate conservative recomputation through an update law, a Gibbs spectral gap, and an observable proxy. Their cycle example uses a uniform length-\(L\) block and a padded deleted set, yielding a scaled Newey--West estimator. Their Fourier formula expresses oracle inflation as an update-set intersection probability divided by the spectral gap, and their monotonicity result only treats contiguous blocks. Their proxy section states that larger update sets delete more outcomes and make approximation harder. Their discussion calls selection of \(L\) and conservativeness in probability open. Harshaw, Middleton, and S\u00e4vje (2026) study admissibility among quadratic variance bounds, which is qualitative and exposure-model dependent rather than a radius-indexed inflation rate. Lin and Ding (2025) prove pointwise design-based HAC conservativeness and asymptotic normality for time-series regressions. Gao and Ding (2025) obtain conservative network-HAC adjustment but not minimax bandwidth choice. K\u00fcnsch (1989) is the moving-block jackknife ancestor. Polyanskiy and Wu (2026) provide a dual Le Cam route for a functional lower bound with rich nuisance variation. No located result proves a lower bound over all assignment-independent update laws and all schedule-uniform observable proxies.

conservative recomputation through an update law, a Gibbs spectral gap, and an observable proxy. Their cycle example uses a uniform length-\\(L\\) block and a padded deleted set, yielding a scaled Newey--West estimator. Their Fourier formula expresses oracle inflation as an update-set intersection probability divided by the spectral gap, and their monotonicity result only treats contiguous blocks. Their proxy section states that larger update sets delete more outcomes and make approximation harder. Their discussion calls selection of \\(L\\) and conservativeness in probability open. Harshaw, Middleton, and S\\u00e4vje (2026) study admissibility among quadratic variance bounds, which is qualitative and exposure-model dependent rather than a radius-indexed inflation rate. Lin and Ding (2025) prove pointwise design-based HAC conservativeness and asymptotic normality for time-series regressions. Gao and Ding (2025) obtain conservative network-HAC adjustment but not minimax bandwidth choice. K\\u00fcnsch (1989) is the moving-block jackknife ancestor. Polyanskiy and Wu (2026) provide a dual Le Cam route for a functional lower bound with rich nuisance variation. No located result proves a lower bound over all assignment-independent update laws and all schedule-uniform observable proxies.","cluster":"experimentation","novelty_justification":{"repo_axis":"The nearest in-repository lines are the downgraded effective-exposure lower-bound proposal and the ego-cluster completion-envelope proposal. banked_reason: The unrestricted all-rule frontier collapses to one on an admissible boundary class, refuting the proposed positive square-root excess lower bound.
